The [[Pii system/Legends|Pii system's]] '''first planet''' was a [[Fire/Legends|fiery]] ball of [[Mineral/Legends|rock]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Pii sun]]. The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was located in the [[Arkanis sector/Legends|Arkanis sector]]<ref name="Greel" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas" /> The planet was a seven-[[Standard day/Legends|day]] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] journey away from the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]], like the rest of the Pii system,<ref name="Greel" /> and along the [[Old Corellian Run]] and the [[Trans Gulf Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]].<ref name="Atlas" />

The planet, along with the rest of the Pii system, was originally located on the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]]. When that [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route changed its course<ref name="Atlas" /> at some point between [[11,000 BBY]] and [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]]<ref name="OldCorellianRunDate" /> and as a result no longer ran through the Pii system, the planet remained on the Old Corellian Run.<ref name="Atlas" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Pii system's first planet was first mentioned in "[[The Greel Wood Haven]]," an article written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]] and featured in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|sixth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] magazine that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[May]] of [[1995]] for their ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Greel" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Pii system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square R-16.<ref name="Atlas" />
